phase1.explanation:
Maps store key-value pairs:

HashMap:
• No guaranteed order
• O(1) average for get/put/containsKey
• Allows one null key, multiple null values
• Not synchronized

TreeMap:
• Sorted by key (natural order or comparator)
• O(log n) for get/put/containsKey
• No null keys (throws NPE)

Common operations:
• put(key, value) — adds or updates
• get(key) — returns value or null
• getOrDefault(key, defaultValue) — returns default if key missing
• containsKey(key) — checks if key exists
• containsValue(value) — checks if value exists
• remove(key) — removes entry
• keySet() — returns Set of keys
• values() — returns Collection of values
• entrySet() — returns Set of entries

Iterating:
for (Map.Entry<K, V> entry : map.entrySet()) {
    K key = entry.getKey();
    V value = entry.getValue();
}

TreeMap-specific:
• firstKey() / lastKey() — smallest/largest key
• floorKey(key) — greatest key <= key
• ceilingKey(key) — smallest key >= key
• headMap(key) — keys < key
• tailMap(key) — keys >= key

When to use HashMap:
• Fast lookup
• No need for order

When to use TreeMap:
• Need sorted keys
• Need range queries

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates a HashMap<String, String> with countries and capitals:
   - "USA" -> "Washington D.C."
   - "France" -> "Paris"
   - "Japan" -> "Tokyo"
   - "Brazil" -> "Brasília"
2. Prints all entries
3. Gets the capital of "France"
4. Gets the capital of "Germany" using getOrDefault (default: "Unknown")
5. Removes "Brazil"
6. Prints all keys
7. Prints all values
8. Checks if "USA" is a key
9. Creates a TreeMap from the HashMap and prints it (sorted)
phase3.openingDialogue: Maps — find your values by their keys! 🗺️
